fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity nebraska, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to nebraska, respiratory therapist compact nebraska, nebraska respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Nebraska DHHS — Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
board.mailing_address:
Nebraska DHHS
Licensure Unit
P.O. Box 94986
Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) Examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Nebraska.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Visit the Nebraska DHHS Online Application Portal at https://ne.gov/go/DHHSlicensure and click 'New User Registration' to create your applicant account with a valid email address and password.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Log into your newly created account on the Nebraska DHHS Online Application Portal and select to start a new application for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license.

steps.title: Complete Application and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form. This includes personal, education, and work history information. Upload necessary supporting documents such as official transcripts and verification of NBRC certification.

steps.title: Arrange for License Verification (for Endorsement)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, ensure official verification of your current/previous licenses from other states are sent directly from those state boards to the Nebraska DHHS Licensure Unit.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee through the secure payment system within the Nebraska DHHS Online Application Portal.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application and respond to any requests for additional information or documentation through your account in the Nebraska DHHS Online Application Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Nebraska. To work in Nebraska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Nebraska
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Nebraska, create an account and submit your application through the Nebraska DHHS Online Application Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'New User Registration,' enter your email, create a password and security questions, agree to the terms, verify your email address, and then log in to start a new application.
